title: Unlocking UK house-building with AI-accelerated planning
gist: Google DeepMind is partnering with the UK government to build a Gemini-powered planning prototype aimed at cutting householder planning-application decision times by 50%, building on the Extract tool already rolled out to English councils, with national availability targeted for 2027 to help deliver the UK's 1.5 million new homes by 2029.

themes.theme: AI planning tool and house-building targets

insights.claim: Google DeepMind is partnering with the UK government on a Gemini-powered planning prototype with the goal of cutting householder planning-application decision times by 50%, supporting the UK target of 1.5 million new homes by 2029.
insights.speaker: Owen Larter and David Thacker
insights.so_what: A concrete government-AI deployment with a 50% efficiency target signals expanding public-sector demand for Gemini-class tools.
insights.quote: Our goal is to help officers cut application decision times by 50%, freeing up time for planners so that more homes can be built.
insights.locator: Unlocking UK house-building with AI-accelerated planning

insights.claim: Householder applications account for nearly 70% of UK planning applications each year, and officers currently spend hours per application cross-referencing policy documents, historical files and PDFs, creating a major bottleneck.
insights.speaker: Owen Larter and David Thacker
insights.so_what: Quantifies the addressable volume for AI automation in UK planning, the bulk of casework is routine and automatable.
insights.quote: householder applications account for nearly 70% of planning applications each year
insights.locator: For a typical new planning application

insights.claim: The prototype is being co-developed with Google Cloud, Faculty, and local planning authorities in Barnet, Dorset and Camden, with the government planning national availability to all councils from 2027.
insights.speaker: Owen Larter and David Thacker
insights.so_what: A 2027 national rollout timeline marks the scaling path from pilot to country-wide government deployment.
insights.quote: the government plans for the new AI planning tool to be made available to all councils nationally from 2027
insights.locator: Following early trials in Barnet, Camden and Dorset

insights.claim: The planning prototype keeps the officer as final decision-maker, recording its work at every step to create a chain of thought and audit trail, while automating data consolidation, local-policy identification, consultation-feedback summarization, and first-draft assessments.
insights.speaker: Owen Larter and David Thacker
insights.so_what: Human-in-the-loop plus audit trails is the template for accountable public-sector AI adoption, lowering deployment friction.
insights.quote: the planning officer remains in full control as the final decision-maker. They review every line the tool generates, edit the reasoning and retain the authority to approve or reject an application.
insights.locator: Crucially, the planning officer remains in full control

insights.claim: Extract, a Gemini-built tool that turns hundreds of pages of legacy planning PDFs into usable data in minutes, was made available to every council in England earlier this month after trials across more than 20 local planning authorities and is expected to save the average council around 255 hours of manual work a year.
insights.speaker: Owen Larter and David Thacker
insights.so_what: A concrete 255-hour-per-council productivity figure is the proof point for AI ROI in government document workflows.
insights.quote: Extract has already shown great success in trials across more than 20 local planning authorities — and is expected to save the average council around 255 hours of manual work a year.
insights.locator: Scaling Extract: Turning legacy documents into usable data
